_comment: Paperclip benchmark #15 — active model evaluator. Per-role suites -> multi-model runner -> deterministic+LLM-judge scorer -> quality-per-token -> per-role model recommendation that drives data-driven tiering (#9). Models are invoked via their CLIs in a neutralized temp CWD (no project CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/rules) so we measure the base model, not the local agent's tools/skills (that is #16's job).
_models_catalog_comment: Validated within-family variants (confirmed callable via their CLI 2026-06-14). NOT in the default sweep — `bench.py all` runs `models` above. Opt in per run: `bench.py all --models claude-haiku,gpt-5.4-mini,gemini-flash,grok-4-fast`. These are the 'lower model' candidates for the cheap-model-+-skill gap-closing strategy. gpt-5.3-codex-spark IS a valid model (same as Paperclip's spark lane, invoked --model gpt-5.3-codex-spark with reasoning_effort=high) but is DAILY RATE-LIMITED — sweeps including it fail with a usage-limit error while exhausted (resets ~mid-afternoon). Genuinely NOT supported on this ChatGPT account (400 invalid_request, never works): plain gpt-5.3, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5-codex.

models_catalog.label: Codex GPT-5.3 Spark
models_catalog.note: Paperclip's cheap codex lane; daily rate-limited

judge._comment: Judge = claude-opus (user authorized heavy claude use 2026-06-14). Sharpest available grader and most neutral (it is a contestant too, but the judge is blind to which model produced an output and applied uniformly, so rankings are fair; its mild self-preference is the one caveat). The gemini-judge baseline (run-20260614-044003) is archived for a cross-judge agreement check. Swap back to {"id":"gemini-pro","adapter":"gemini","model_arg":null} to conserve claude quota.

paperclip._comment: Agentic 'Paperclip function' lane (paperclip_lane.py). OPT-IN: never part of a default `all` sweep — run with `--roles paperclip --models grok-4.3,grok-4-fast,grok-4.1-fast`. Requires PAPERCLIP_API_URL + PAPERCLIP_API_KEY (board token) in env. Maps each benchmark model id -> a DEDICATED bench AGENT that runs that model; the lane creates a fixture issue assigned to that agent, triggers a fresh-session run, and scores whether it reaches a VALID disposition. SAFE-BY-DEFAULT: `agents` is empty so a stray run is a no-op (the lane skips models with no configured agent). DO NOT point this at live fleet agents — that commandeers real lanes. Provision dedicated throwaway bench Hermes agents (one per grok model) in a sandbox/bench company, then fill `agents` + `benchCompanyId`. Candidate models to bench: grok-4.3 (reasoning, the working baseline), grok-4-fast / grok-4.1-fast (the deployed non-reasoning variants under suspicion), grok-4.20. For reference, the live TSC (211e0f96) agents on these models are MIDAS-Hermes=8e90bc2f (grok-4.3), Compiler-Hermes=94d53f33 (grok-4-fast-non-reasoning), Engineer-Hermes=a201c6a0 (grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning) — use these ONLY to model dedicated bench agents after them, not as bench subjects.
paperclip._runtime_comment: Agentic cells are heavy multi-step LIVE runs against the shared server (same box as the live fleet + DB). Keep maxWorkers low (2) so the sweep doesn't hammer the fleet, and cellTimeoutSec high (600) because complex stages (child+blocker+plan+approval) legitimately take minutes. The lane retries transient timeouts so a slow response doesn't fail a cell.

scoring._comment: Per-task blend. If a task has only deterministic checks (e.g. intake ground-truth), quality = deterministic. If only judge criteria, quality = judge. If both, quality = normalized weighted blend of the two.

recommendation._comment: objective=peak_quality_cost_aware (davin's policy 2026-06-14): TIER FOR PEAK QUALITY, but don't pay multiples for marginal gains. Rule per role: start from the highest-quality model; among models within quality_epsilon (0.02) of it, pick the one with the LOWEST mean output tokens — BUT only drop from the peak if the cheaper model is materially cheaper (peak_output / cheaper_output >= cost_ratio_trigger, default 1.5x). Otherwise keep the peak. Report shows peak, the cost-aware pick, quality given up, and the output-cost ratio so the tradeoff is explicit. Set quality_epsilon=0 to always take true peak. value_metric=output because total tokens are ~95% fixed CLI overhead (a harness artifact).

run._comment: Each model call runs in a fresh empty temp CWD with config/rules-ignore flags where the CLI supports them. max_workers parallelizes across (role,task,model) cells; keep modest — the Mac is shared with the live fleet + image-gen.
